step_by_step_thinking:
The key information here pertains to the derivation of the 4th pharyngeal arch artery, specifically focusing on what develops from its left and right components. From the articles cited, we understand that the left 4th pharyngeal arch gives rise primarily to parts of the left common carotid artery and subsequently influences the left subclavian artery, contributing indirectly to the left aortic arch. Conversely, the right 4th pharyngeal arch plays a role mainly in developing the proximal aspect of the right subclavian artery. Given all this context, none of the listed options accurately reflect solely those specific arteries developed exclusively from either component of the 4th pharyngeal arch.
\boxed{\text{D}}
